title: Marsdenia lloydii
intro: Marsdenia lloydii, commonly known as Lloyd's vine or Llave's vine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Marsdenia lloydii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Marsdenia lloydii is often used to prepare various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to support the immune system and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
taxonomy: Marsdenia lloydii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the genus Marsdenia, which is within the family Apocynaceae, also known as the dogbane or oleander family. This genus comprises around 120 species of evergreen or deciduous vines. Marsdenia lloydii is native to China and is often found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant's classification reflects its botanical and evolutionary relationships within the Apocynaceae family. The specific classification of Marsdenia lloydii is Marsdenia lloydii (Tsiang) P. T.
